nodes.label: P17_T001
nodes.identity_summary: This person is a research-driven experimenter who prioritizes fragrance and friend recommendations above all else, with strong preference for Indian D2C brands. They have moderate brand loyalty but actively seek new options, always checking reviews before purchase and willing to pay premiums for meaningful benefits like long-lasting fragrance or cruelty-free formulations.

nodes.label: fragrance-first-decision-maker
nodes.description: Fragrance is the primary driver of body wash selection, with specific preference for floral and herbal/earthy scents that provide self-confidence when stepping out.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Smell triggered the switch; bored of same bodywash used for 2 years
[REAL] What would make you switch: better_smell, influencer, friend_rec
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? Fragrance, color, and bottle design
[SYNTHETIC] Not try it — fragrance is non-negotiable in body wash
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely focus first on the scent description and would reject any unscented options regardless of other benefits.

nodes.label: friend-family-recommendation-prioritizer
nodes.description: Rates friends and family as 4/5 for trust in personal care decisions, significantly higher than any other information source including dermatologists or online reviews.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust: Friends and family: 4, Dermatologists: 2, Online reviews: 3
[REAL] If a friend recommends a body wash to you, how likely are you to try it: 4
[SYNTHETIC] Your mother's suggestion of a traditional brand... I trust friends and family recommendations the most
[SYNTHETIC] My closest friend. I trust friends and family recommendations most
nodes.predictive_value: When shown marketing featuring friend testimonials or word-of-mouth campaigns, this person would likely respond more positively than to expert endorsements or clinical claims.

nodes.label: indian-d2c-brand-preferrer
nodes.description: Explicitly prefers Indian brands, particularly D2C Indian brands, and would choose them over international options even at similar price points.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Prefer Indian brands, particularly D2C Indian brands
[REAL] Made in India: 10_20% premium willing to pay
[SYNTHETIC] New-age Indian D2C brands: 4 points, Legacy Indian brands: 3 points, International premium brands: 2 points
[SYNTHETIC] Made in India at ₹249. I prefer Indian brands
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a choice between Indian D2C and international brands at similar prices, this person would likely choose the Indian option and respond well to 'Made in India' messaging.

nodes.label: mandatory-review-checker
nodes.description: Always checks online reviews before buying personal care products, specifically preferring Reddit for honest opinions and looking at review count as a primary factor.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you read online reviews before buying personal care products? Reddit
[REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? price, review_count, images
[SYNTHETIC] I always check reviews before trying unknown brands, even in urgent situations
[SYNTHETIC] 12,000 reviews give me much better data to work with than 85 reviews
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product launch, this person would likely delay purchase until sufficient reviews accumulate and would be skeptical of products with few reviews regardless of rating.

nodes.label: comfortable-new-brand-experimenter
nodes.description: Rates comfort with trying new brands as 5/5 and actively rotates between multiple brands (Lux, Pears, Fiama) rather than sticking to one.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands: 5
[REAL] What body wash brands do you currently use? Lux, Pears, Fiama
[SYNTHETIC] I'm comfortable trying new brands, and the guarantee makes the higher price feel safer
[SYNTHETIC] I already rotate between Lux, Pears, and Fiama
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new brand launches or limited editions, this person would likely be among early adopters, especially if reviews or guarantees reduce perceived risk.

nodes.label: brand-website-direct-buyer

evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? brand_website
[REAL] Do you use quick commerce? Don't use quick commerce... Trust other shopping methods more
[SYNTHETIC] I prefer buying from brand websites and usually get the best product range there
[SYNTHETIC] Direct brand website link. Prefer supporting D2C brands directly
nodes.predictive_value: When shown purchase options, this person would likely choose brand direct-to-consumer channels over Amazon or quick commerce, even if prices are slightly higher.

nodes.label: cruelty-free-unlimited-premium-payer

[SYNTHETIC] Stop buying immediately. Cruelty-free is unlimited premium for me - it's a complete dealbreaker
[SYNTHETIC] Check if it's cruelty-free since that's unlimited premium for me
[SYNTHETIC] I can't support dishonest brands. I care about cruelty-free and honest practices
nodes.predictive_value: When shown products without clear cruelty-free certification, this person would likely reject them entirely, but would pay any premium for verified cruelty-free options.

nodes.label: texture-dissatisfied-seeker

links.mechanism: Since fragrance is the primary decision driver, they're willing to pay significantly more for products that deliver superior scent longevity, seeing it as core value rather than a nice-to-have feature.
High trust in personal recommendations creates a framework for valuing authentic user experiences, which extends to seeking out honest community reviews on Reddit rather than trusting brand claims.
Preference for Indian D2C brands naturally leads to buying directly from brand websites where these companies typically offer their full range and best customer experience.
High comfort with trying new brands combines with practical risk management through trial sizes, allowing experimentation while minimizing financial risk.
Strong ethical stance on animal testing creates appreciation for brands that are transparent about their practices and values, building trust through openness rather than vague claims.
Tendency to rotate between brands and switch based on mood creates strong preference for purchase flexibility over subscription commitments that lock in specific products.
Limited budget forces careful evaluation of what premiums are truly worth paying, leading to willingness to pay extra only for benefits that provide clear functional or ethical value.
Preference for clinical validation drives deeper research into ingredients and scientific claims rather than accepting traditional wisdom or marketing statements at face value.
Context-dependent grooming approach specifically focuses on social situations where fragrance becomes a confidence tool, driving strategic use of scented products for impression management.
While wanting simple routines, they're simultaneously particular about texture quality, creating tension between simplicity preference and specific performance expectations.
The obsession with fragrance quality stems from using scent as a confidence-building tool for social interactions, making fragrance longevity personally meaningful rather than just preferential.
The high priority on fragrance quality manifests in specific scent preferences for herbal and earthy profiles that align with personal taste and confidence needs.
Skepticism about speed claims reflects a broader pattern of evaluating innovations based on practical relevance rather than accepting novel features at face value.
Price sensitivity drives preference for trial sizes as a risk mitigation strategy, allowing product testing without full financial commitment.
Dissatisfaction with current options drives the behavior of trying multiple brands within short timeframes, seeking better alternatives that meet fragrance and texture expectations.
Both traits reflect a preference for individual-focused solutions while maintaining control over personal information and product customization.
The research-oriented approach includes evaluating how well new concepts align with established brand positioning, using brand consistency as a quality signal.
The preference for functional routines drives interest in products that combine multiple benefits, eliminating separate moisturizing steps for efficiency.
Concern about skin irritation reinforces the need for trusted brands with proven gentle formulations, making brand reputation a safety signal.
Value consciousness drives appreciation for subscription models that offer better cost-per-use ratios compared to one-time premium purchases.
